criteria.label: Define model complexity and its role in the bias–variance tradeoff.
criteria.example: Write: complexity is capacity (depth, degree, width); increasing it lowers bias and usually raises variance.

criteria.label: Sketch or describe the U-shaped validation error vs complexity curve.
criteria.example: Draw error on y-axis, complexity on x-axis; label underfitting left, sweet spot middle, overfitting right.

criteria.label: Diagnose whether to increase or decrease complexity from train vs val errors.
criteria.example: Given train MSE 0.9 and val MSE 0.95, recommend increasing depth; given train 0.02 and val 0.5, recommend reducing degree.

criteria.label: Name complexity knobs for at least two model families.
criteria.example: List polynomial degree for regression and max_depth for decision trees; optionally k for kNN.

criteria.label: Explain why effective complexity can differ from parameter count.
criteria.example: Describe Ridge on high-degree poly: many coefficients but alpha=100 behaves like low-degree on CV.
